text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okonkwo, Renata
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
SUBMITTING SERVICE: Gynecologic Oncology
CLINICAL HISTORY: 40-year-old female with history of Stage IIB (ypT1b ypN1 M0 post-neoadjuvant) triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, Grade 3, treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab + dose-dense AC followed by paclitaxel + pembrolizumab, followed by bilateral nipple-sparing mastectomy with left axillary lymph node dissection and adjuvant capecitabine + pembrolizumab (total 1 year). Confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5074del; p.Cys1692ValfsTer17). Presenting for risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy.
PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: BRCA1 germline carrier; risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: See final diagnosis below.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Right fallopian tube and ovary
  B. Left fallopian tube and ovary
  C. Peritoneal washings
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED - TO BE POPULATED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Laparoscopic risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
PROCEDURE: Laparoscopic bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

SPECIMEN A - RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY:
Received fresh, labeled "right fallopian tube and ovary," is a fallopian tube measuring 6.8 cm in length x 0.5 cm in diameter with a fimbriated end intact, attached to an ovary measuring 3.1 x 2.0 x 1.4 cm. The external surface of the ovary is smooth, gray-tan, and glistening. On serial sectioning, the ovarian parenchyma is homogeneous, tan-yellow, and firm with multiple small follicles ranging from 0.2 to 0.6 cm and one corpus luteum measuring 0.9 cm with a central golden-yellow zone. No discrete nodules, cysts greater than 1 cm, or areas of hemorrhage or necrosis are identified. The fallopian tube is serially sectioned at 2 mm intervals. The fimbriated end is amputated and submitted as a separate section per the SEE-FIM protocol. The tubal lumen is patent. The mucosa is tan-pink and unremarkable without papillary excrescences or mural nodularity. No adhesions. Representative sections submitted per SEE-FIM protocol.

Cassettes A1-A3: Right fimbriated end, serially sectioned, all submitted (SEE-FIM)
Cassette A4-A5: Right fallopian tube, mid and isthmic sections
Cassette A6-A8: Right ovary, all of tissue submitted in representative sections

SPECIMEN B - LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY:
Received fresh, labeled "left fallopian tube and ovary," is a fallopian tube measuring 7.1 cm in length x 0.6 cm in diameter with a fimbriated end intact, attached to an ovary measuring 2.8 x 1.9 x 1.3 cm. The external surface of the ovary is smooth and gray-tan. On serial sectioning, the ovarian parenchyma is homogeneous, tan-yellow, and firm with several small follicles ranging from 0.2 to 0.5 cm. No corpus luteum identified. No discrete nodules, cysts greater than 1 cm, hemorrhage, or necrosis. The fallopian tube is serially sectioned at 2 mm intervals. The fimbriated end is amputated and submitted in its entirety per the SEE-FIM protocol. The tubal lumen is patent. The mucosa is tan-pink and unremarkable without papillary excrescences or mural nodularity. No adhesions or peritoneal implants identified on the external serosal surface.

Cassettes B1-B3: Left fimbriated end, serially sectioned, all submitted (SEE-FIM)
Cassette B4-B5: Left fallopian tube, mid and isthmic sections
Cassette B6-B7: Left ovary, all of tissue submitted in representative sections

SPECIMEN C - PERITONEAL WASHINGS:
Received as a fluid specimen labeled "peritoneal washings." Approximately 30 mL of slightly turbid, light-yellow fluid. Cytospin and cell block prepared and submitted.

Cassette C1: Peritoneal washing cytospin
Cassette C2: Peritoneal washing cell block

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

SPECIMEN A - RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY:
Fallopian tube (SEE-FIM protocol): Serial sections of the fimbriated end and entire fallopian tube examined. The tubal epithelium consists of normal ciliated columnar and secretory cells without cytologic atypia. No increase in p53 expression on immunohistochemistry (p53 staining pattern: wild-type, scattered positive cells with no aberrant diffuse or complete null staining pattern). No loss of PAX8 expression. Cell proliferation index by Ki-67 is low (less than 10% of epithelial cells). No secretory cell outgrowths (SCOUTs) identified meeting criteria for concern. No serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). No evidence of invasive carcinoma. No tubal intraepithelial neoplasia.

Ovary: Normal premenopausal ovarian parenchyma. Multiple primordial, primary, and secondary follicles identified in the cortical stroma. One corpus luteum with central fibrotic zone. Ovarian stroma unremarkable without stromal hyperplasia, thecomatosis, or hilar cell proliferations. No endometriosis. No surface epithelial inclusions with atypia. No granulosa cell proliferations. No evidence of malignancy.

SPECIMEN B - LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY:
Fallopian tube (SEE-FIM protocol): Serial sections of the fimbriated end and entire fallopian tube examined. The tubal epithelium is normal in appearance with ciliated columnar and secretory (PAX8-positive) cells without cytologic atypia. p53 immunohistochemistry demonstrates wild-type staining pattern (scattered low-level positivity). Ki-67 proliferation index low (less than 10% of tubal epithelial cells). No secretory cell outgrowths (SCOUTs) of significance. No serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). No tubal intraepithelial neoplasia. No evidence of invasive carcinoma.

Ovary: Normal premenopausal ovarian parenchyma. Multiple small follicles in various stages of development. Ovarian cortex and medulla without significant pathologic change. No surface implants. No endometriosis. No malignancy identified.

SPECIMEN C - PERITONEAL WASHINGS:
Cytospin preparation and cell block reviewed. Mesothelial cells present in small groups with normal cytomorphology. Inflammatory cells including lymphocytes and rare macrophages. No atypical epithelial cells. No malignant cells identified.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

Immunohistochemistry (performed on fimbriated end sections, Specimens A and B):
  - p53 (DO-7): Wild-type staining pattern bilaterally. No aberrant diffuse strong nuclear overexpression. No complete absence (null pattern). Negative for p53 aberrant staining indicative of TP53 mutation in the tubal epithelium.
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Less than 10% nuclear staining in tubal epithelial cells bilaterally. Low proliferation index.
  - PAX8: Diffuse nuclear positivity in secretory tubal epithelial cells, as expected. No loss of PAX8.

Note: p53 immunohistochemistry is routinely performed in BRCA1/2 carriers undergoing RRSO to evaluate for occult serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC). Aberrant p53 (diffuse strong overexpression or null staining) would prompt further investigation. In this case, wild-type p53 staining is reassuring.

Cytology (Specimen C): No malignant cells identified. See cytopathology report [accession number on file] for detailed interpretation.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY (laparoscopic excision):
   - No serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC)
   - No tubal dysplasia or intraepithelial neoplasia
   - Normal premenopausal ovarian parenchyma; no evidence of malignancy
   - Wild-type p53 immunostaining pattern in tubal epithelium
   - SEE-FIM protocol applied; entire fimbriated end examined

B. LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE AND OVARY (laparoscopic excision):
   - No serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC)
   - No tubal dysplasia or intraepithelial neoplasia
   - Normal premenopausal ovarian parenchyma; no evidence of malignancy
   - Wild-type p53 immunostaining pattern in tubal epithelium
   - SEE-FIM protocol applied; entire fimbriated end examined

C. PERITONEAL WASHINGS:
   - Negative for malignant cells
   - Reactive mesothelial cells and inflammatory cells only

OVERALL SUMMARY: Risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy in a BRCA1 germline carrier. No occult malignancy, STIC, or premalignant lesion identified in either specimen. Findings are consistent with normal premenopausal ovarian and tubal tissue. SEE-FIM protocol satisfied for both fallopian tubes.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING: Not applicable (no malignancy identified).

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The SEE-FIM (Sectioning and Extensively Examining the Fimbriated End) protocol was applied to both fallopian tube specimens per standard of care for BRCA1/2 germline carriers undergoing risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy. This protocol involves complete submission of the fimbriated end in 2 mm serial sections, which is the site of highest risk for occult serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) in BRCA1 mutation carriers. p53 immunohistochemistry was performed as part of this protocol. No STIC or occult carcinoma was identified. These findings are consistent with a negative RRSO and should be interpreted in the clinical context of ongoing surveillance.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED BY: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Division of Gynecologic and Breast Pathology
Department of Pathology

ELECTRONICALLY VERIFIED AND SIGNED
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
